MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or, In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication
Off Calendar Sep-20-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES:
Geoff T. Sloniker of BRAYTON & PURCELL (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiffs / opposing parties James Carter and Donna Carter

RULING:
DEFENDANT HONEYWELL INTERNATIONAL INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or, IN THE ALTERNATIVE, SUMMARY ADJUDICATION

No other appearance. No objection made by plaintiffs and the Court adopted its tentative ruling: Defendant Honeywell International Inc.'s motion for summary judgment is denied and its alternate motion for summary adjudication of plaintiffs' claim for punitive damages is granted. As to summary judgment, defendant failed to sustain its burden of demonstrating that plaintiffs do not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that Mr. Carter was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials attributable to defendant. Defendant concedes that plaintiffs identified Henry "Tom" Havener as a witness regarding Mr. Carter's exposure to Bendix brakes. (UMF 4.) Defendant's failure to address Mr. Havener's knowledge precludes defendant from successfully invoking the factually devoid prong of Aguilar v. Atlantic Richfield Co. (2001) 25 Cal.4th 826. As to summary adjudication, defendant sustained its initial burden and plaintiffs failed to present evidence sufficient to create a triable issue whether defendant acted with the requisite malice, fraud or oppression to warrant the imposition of punitive damages. While plaintiffs do not need to prove their case for punitive damages at the summary adjudication stage, the Court takes the higher proof standard into consideration and concludes that no reasonable jury could find plaintiffs' evidence to be clear and convincing proof of malice, fraud or oppression. (See American Airlines, Inc. v. Sheppard, Mullin, Richter & Hampton (2002) 96 Cal.App.4th 1017; Hoch v. Allied-Signal, Inc. (1994) 24 Cal.App.4th 48.) Plaintiffs' objections nos. 1 and 2 are sustained and objection no. 3 is overruled as to "No questions about Honeywell or Bendix were asked at the deposition and there was no Bendix identification by Mr. Orona" and sustained as to the remainder. Defendant's objections to plaintiffs' evidence are sustained as to Exhibits C, F, I, J, K, M, S, T, V, W, Y, AA, DD, EE, FF, GG, HH, II, JJ, KK and NN and overruled as to Exhibits A, B, E, G, H, L, N, O, P, Q, R, U, X, Z, BB, CC, LL, MM, OO and PP. PLAINTIFF'S MOTION FOR ORDER GRANTING PREFERENCE IN SETTING CASE FOR TRIAL, AND EXTENDING DISCOVERY CUTOFF

Hearing held and tentative ruling adopted as amended: Plaintiffs' motion for preference is granted pursuant to C.C.P. 36(e). The trial date is September 25, 2017 at 11:15am in Department 503. Last day pursuant to C.C.P. 36(f) is October 6, 2017. Local Rule 20 shall be followed by the parties as well as all San Francisco Local Rules and California Rules of Court. The last day for hearing summary judgment/adjudication motions is September 19, 2017. Summary judgment/adjudication motions shall be brought on regular notice pursuant to the relevant provisions of the C.C.P., unless the parties stipulate otherwise. The Court will allow a maximum of four summary judgment/adjudication motions per day to be calendared in this action. Before a party files and serves such motion, it must contact the Court's Calendar Clerk to make a reservation. Time to respond to written discovery that has not yet been served is shortened to 20 days. For written discovery that has already been served, responses are due within 20 days of this hearing or by the date determined by the C.C.P., whichever is earlier. If there is any kind of issue/dispute that requires meet and confer, such meet and confer shall not be made via email or letter. It shall occur in person or via telephone. Electronic service is considered the equivalent of personal service. Plaintiffs to make a settlement demand on defendants no later than August 11, 2017. Responses to the demand are due no later than August 25, 2017. The parties are ordered to provide confidential settlement demands stating their best and final offers. These confidential demands must be submitted to Pang Ly by September 18, 2017 at 2:00p.m. Settlement conference in room 603 at 9:30a.m. on September 21, 2017. Fact discovery cut-off date is September 8, 2017. Expert discovery cut-off date is September 22, 2017. All bankruptcy documents shall be turned over to defendants no later than July 7, 2017. If plaintiffs submit documents to bankruptcy trusts after this date, they must notify defendants of this no later than five days after submission.
